content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN. Consider the sequence containing the integers 1,2,…,N1, 2, \ldots, N1,2,…,N in increasing order (each exactly once). Find the maximum length of its contiguous subsequence with an even sum.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer --- the maximum length of a contiguous subsequence with even sum.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the maximum possible length of a contiguous subarray of [1,2,…,N] whose sum is even.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N=3, total sum 6 is even → answer 3. For N=4, total sum 10 is even → answer 4. For N=5, total sum 15 is odd → drop one odd element → answer 4.

solution.approach: The sum of the whole array 1..N is S = N*(N+1)/2. If S is even we can take the whole array, so answer = N. If S is odd we must remove at least one element with odd value to make the remaining sum even. The smallest odd element is 1, which is always present, so we can drop exactly one element and obtain length N-1. Hence answer = N when S is even, otherwise N-1. The parity of S is even iff N mod 4 is 0 or 3. Therefore answer = N if N%4==0 or N%4==3, else N-1.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, total O(T).

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    t = int(data[0])
    out = []
    for i in range(1, t+1):
        n = int(data[i])
        ans = n if n%4==0 or n%4==3 else n-1
        out.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
